channel.title: Github Actions on Everything under the Cloud
channel.description: Recent content in Github Actions on Everything under the Cloud
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: Monolithic Terraform Is a Trap
item.description:
The Problem

One state file. Four environments. Hundreds of AWS resources all crammed together. And for a while, it worked fine. Until it didn’t.

The problem with monolithic state isn’t that it breaks immediately. It quietly accumulates risk. Every terraform plan refreshes your entire infrastructure across every environment at once. Every terraform apply is one misread output away from touching production when you only meant to change dev. There’s no blast radius control. No safety net. Just one big trust fall with your cloud account.

item.title: Automating Security Visibility
item.description:
The Problem

So our team was shipping code constantly, but we had no real way to know if critical vulnerabilities were in it. Scanning was this manual thing that happened randomly—basically whenever someone remembered to do it. You’d find out about issues weeks later, or the worst case, right before you needed to deploy something.

Not ideal.

How I Built It

I set up three workflows that talk to each other. Here’s how it works:
